items.title: Score Samsung’s 27-inch Odyssey G50D QHD 180Hz gaming monitor for your battlestation while it’s down to $200 today ($150 off)
items.summary:
Today only, as part of its Deals of the Day offers, Best Buy has listed Samsung’s 27-inch Odyssey G50D QHD gaming monitor for $199.99 shipped. This is regularly a $350 display, which is currently going for $260 directly at Samsung. Amazon currently has it for $250, but Best Buy’s offer beats that by an additional $50 today, marking one of the lowest prices we have tracked for it to date. Only once before today have we seen this display go for less, and it’s now within just $10 of that all-time low. It’s a pretty good option to consider if you’re looking to upgrade from a 1080p monitor and don’t want to spend a ton on your first QHD display. Head below for more details.

items.summary:
As part of a wider sale on chargers, Woot is offering Anker’s 3-in-1 15W charging cube for $64.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise). Originally priced at $150, this charger has generally hovered between $90 to a $100 range at Amazon. It briefly dropped to the $79 mark earlier this year, but today’s offer goes even lower to land it $15 below that price. This is the lowest we have seen this charger drop to date, and it’s now beating its previous all-time low by $5. It’s a pretty good option to consider if you’re looking to pick up a new multiple device wireless charger for your desk or bedside table, and you can learn more about it in detail below.

items.summary:
Over at Amazon, you can pick up a two-pack of Eve Aqua (Apple Home) Smart Water Controllers for $189.99 shipped, which beats out the single-device package currently keeping to its $150 full price. This occurrence is following in the same manner that we last saw during Black Friday, when it then fell from $650 to $600 at official retailers, and remained there until now. Today, the price is getting another $50 cut, landing it back at its lowest price on Amazon in over a year, which we expect will be the regular going rate going forward, as it is now at this same price on the Tesla site.

Originally priced at $1,400, this ultrawide display has more recently been hovering around the $1,100 mark at Amazon, making this offer a particularly notable one. Today’s deal knocks a massive $442 off its usual going rate, delivering a solid 40% discount and marking the lowest price we’ve tracked for this model so far. While it may not be the cheapest ultrawide gaming monitor on the market, it’s still an excellent value for a curved OLED panel with a 240Hz refresh rate. We recently saw the larger 39-inch version of this monitor hit a new all-time low as well, and now the more compact 34-inch variant is now at a great price for folks looking for a slightly smaller setup.
